content.cn_text: 在今天的课上，我们将讨论印度铁路的历史，从它开始到1945年被政府接管。
content.en_text: In today's lecture, we are going to be talking about the history of Indian railways, from when they began, up until 1945 when they had all been taken over by the government.

content.cn_text: 印度铁路公司是一家印度国有企业，由印度政府通过铁道部拥有和经营。
content.en_text: Indian Railways is an Indian state-owned enterprise, owned and operated by the Government of India through the Ministry of Railways.

content.cn_text: 它是世界上最大的铁路网之一，全长6.5万公里，有11.5万公里的轨道，有7500个车站。
content.en_text: It is one of the world's largest railway networks comprising 115,000 km of track over a route of 65,000 km and there are 7,500 stations.

content.cn_text: 它每天运送超过2500万人次，每年运送超过90亿人次。
content.en_text: It transports over 25 million passengers daily, which is over 9 billion on an annual basis.

content.cn_text: 按员工数量计算，印度铁路公司是世界第九大商业或公用事业雇主，拥有140多万员工。
content.en_text: Indian Railways is the world's ninth largest commercial or utility employer, by number of employees, with over 1.4 million employees.

content.cn_text: 印度铁路运输的历史始于19世纪中期。
content.en_text: The history of rail transport in India began in the mid-nineteenth century.

content.cn_text: 印度修建铁路的压力主要来自伦敦。
content.en_text: The core of the pressure for building railways in India came from London.

content.cn_text: 1848年，印度连一公里铁路线都没有。
content.en_text: In 1848, there was not a single kilometre of railway line in India.

content.cn_text: 从1857年起，英国工程师罗伯特·梅特兰·布里尔顿负责铁路的扩建。
content.en_text: A British engineer, Robert Maitland Brereton, was responsible for the expansion of the railways from 1857 onwards.

content.cn_text: 东印度铁路的阿拉哈巴德-贾巴尔普尔支线于1867年6月开通。
content.en_text: The Allahabad-Jabalpur branch line of the East Indian Railway had been opened in June 1867.

content.cn_text: 布里列顿负责将这条铁路与大印度半岛铁路连接起来，形成了总长6400公里的铁路网络。
content.en_text: Brereton was responsible for linking this with the Great Indian Peninsula Railway, resulting in a combined network of 6,400 km.

content.cn_text: 因此，从孟买直接飞往加尔各答成为可能。
content.en_text: Hence it became possible to travel directly from Bombay to Calcutta.

content.cn_text: 这条路线于1870年3月7日正式开通，它是法国作家儒勒·凡尔纳《八十天环游世界》一书的灵感来源之一。
content.en_text: This route was officially opened on 7 March 1870 and it was part of the inspiration for French writer Jules Verne's book Around the World in Eighty Days.

content.cn_text: 在开幕式上，总督梅奥勋爵总结说，如果可能的话，在尽可能早的时候，全国应该覆盖一个统一系统的线路网络。
content.en_text: At the opening ceremony, the Viceroy Lord Mayo concluded that, if possible, at the earliest possible moment, the whole country should be covered with a network of lines in a uniform system.

content.cn_text: 到1875年，英国公司在印度铁路上投资了大约9500万英镑。
content.en_text: By 1875, about £95 million were invested by British companies in Indian railways.

content.cn_text: 到1880年，铁路网络的路线里程约为14500公里，主要从孟买、马德拉斯和加尔各答这三个主要港口城市向内辐射。
content.en_text: By 1880 the network had a route mileage of about 14,500 km, mostly radiating inward from the three major port cities of Bombay, Madras and Calcutta.

content.cn_text: 到1895年，印度已经开始建造自己的机车，并于1896年派遣工程师和机车帮助建造乌干达铁路。
content.en_text: By 1895, India had started building its own locomotives, and in 1896 sent engineers and locomotives to help build the Uganda Railways.

content.cn_text: 1900年，大印度半岛铁路成为一家国有公司。
content.en_text: In 1900, the Great Indian Peninsula Railway became a government owned company.

content.cn_text: 这个网络扩展到今天的阿萨姆邦、拉贾斯坦邦和安得拉邦，很快，各个自治王国开始拥有自己的铁路系统。
content.en_text: The network spread to the modern day states of Assam, Rajasthan and Andhra Pradesh and soon various autonomous kingdoms began to have their own rail systems.

content.cn_text: 1905年，成立了一个早期的铁路局，但权力正式授予当时的印度总督Curzon勋爵。
content.en_text: In 1905,an early Railway Board was constituted, but the powers were formally vested under Lord Curzon, the then Viceroy of India.

content.cn_text: 它隶属于商务部和工业部，由一名政府铁路官员担任主席，一名来自英国的铁路经理和一家铁路公司的代理人担任其他两名成员。铁路公司有史以来第一次开始盈利。
content.en_text: It served under the Department of Commerce and Industry and had a government railway official serving as chairman, a railway manager from England and an agent of one of the company railways as the other two members.For the first time in its history, the Railways began to make a profit.

content.cn_text: 1907年，几乎所有的铁路公司都被政府接管了。
content.en_text: In 1907 almost all the rail companies were taken over by the government.

content.cn_text: 第二年，第一辆电力机车问世。
content.en_text: The following year, the first electric locomotive made its appearance.

content.cn_text: 随着第一次世界大战的到来，铁路被用来满足英国在印度以外的需求，但随着战争的结束，铁路处于年久失修和崩溃的状态。
content.en_text: With the arrival of World War I,the railways were used to meet the needs of the British outside India, but with the end of the war, the railways were in a state of disrepair and collapse.

content.cn_text: 1920年，随着铁路网扩展到61220公里，英国铁路经济学家威廉·阿克沃斯爵士提出了中央管理的必要性。
content.en_text: In 1920, with the network having expanded to 61,220 km, a need for central management was mooted by Sir William Acworth, a British railway economist.

content.cn_text: 以阿克沃斯为主席的东印度铁路委员会为基础，政府接管了铁路的管理，并将铁路的财政从其他政府收入中分离出来。
content.en_text: Based on the East India Railway Committee chaired by Acworth, the government took over the management of the Railways and detached the finances of the Railways from other governmental revenues.

content.cn_text: 1920年至1929年是经济繁荣时期;全国铁路总里程达6.6万公里;铁路的资本价值约为6.87亿英镑;它们每年运送超过6.2亿乘客和约9000万吨货物。
content.en_text: The period between 1920 and 1929 was a period of economic boom; there were 66,000 km of railway lines serving the country; the railways represented a capital value of some 687 million sterling; and they carried over 620 million passengers and approximately 90 million tons of goods each year.

content.cn_text: 大萧条之后，铁路在接下来的八年里遭受了经济损失，第二次世界大战严重破坏了铁路。
content.en_text: Following the Great Depression, the railways suffered economically for the next eight years and the Second World War severely crippled the railways.

content.cn_text: 从1939年开始，大约40%的铁路车辆，包括机车和客车被带到中东，铁路车间被改造成弹药车间，许多铁路轨道被拆除，以帮助盟军参战。
content.en_text: Starting 1939, about 40% of the rolling stock including locomotives and coaches was taken to the Middle East, the railways workshops were converted to ammunitions workshops and many railway tracks were dismantled to help the Allies in the war.

content.cn_text: 到1946年，所有的铁路系统都被政府接管了。
content.en_text: By 1946 all rail systems had been taken over by the government.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-35
Complete the sentences below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer
groups.collect:
Indian Railways is owned and $15120$ by the government of India.
There are more than $15121$ million people working for Indian Railways
The $15122$ of the railways from 1857 occurred under Robert Maitland
Brereton.
The joining of the East Indian Railway with the Great Indian Peninsula Railway led to a network of $15123$ kilometres.
The route from Bombay to Calcutta, opened in 1870, was an $15124$ for the book Around the World in 80 days.

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解"owned and operated"这个常见的商业短语。题目要求填写一个单词，而原文中明确提到"owned and operated by the Government of India"。由于题干已经包含了"owned"的概念（"Indian Railways is owned ... by the government of India"），所以正确答案应该是"operated"。这里考察了学生对商业用语的理解，以及准确把握题目要求的能力。此外，这个答案也体现了国有企业的特点，即由政府拥有并运营。记住，在这类填空题中，仔细聆听与题干相关的动词是非常重要的。

在这道题中，关键是要准确捕捉与印度铁路员工数量相关的信息。文中明确提到"Indian Railways is the world's ninth largest commercial or utility employer, by number of employees, with over 1.4 million employees"。这里的"1.4 million"就是我们需要的答案。注意题目要求填写的是"ONE WORD ONLY"，所以我们只需要填写数字"1.4"。

要正确回答这个问题，你需要注意以下几点：
1. 不要混淆员工数量与乘客数量。文中也提到了每天运送2500万乘客，每年90亿乘客，这些数字都不是我们要找的。
2. 理解"over"的含义。"over 1.4 million"意味着至少有140万，所以1.4是正确答案。
3. 准确识别"employees"这个词，它与题干中的"people working"相对应。

记住，在雅思听力中，数字信息往往是重要的考点，所以在听到与主题相关的数字时要特别留意。同时，也要注意辨别不同数字所对应的不同信息，避免被干扰信息误导。

这道题的正确答案是"expansion"。文中提到Robert Maitland Brereton负责从1857年开始的铁路扩建。这里的关键在于理解"be responsible for"这个短语，它表示"负责"某事。题目要求填写一个单词，而且必须是名词形式，因为它需要在"the [ ] of the railways"这个结构中填入。"expansion"恰好符合这些要求，它表示"扩张"或"扩建"，与原文中描述的Brereton的职责完全吻合。此外，这个词也符合题目要求的只填一个单词的规定。因此，"expansion"是最合适的答案。注意避免填入"responsible"，因为它是形容词，不适合填入这个名词位置。

这道题的关键在于理解两条铁路线的连接及其结果。文中提到Brereton负责将东印度铁路的Allahabad-Jabalpur支线与大印度半岛铁路连接起来，这个连接的结果就是形成了一个6,400公里的综合网络。题目中问的正是这个连接后的网络长度，因此答案是6400。注意听力中出现的其他数字，如115,000 km或65,000 km，这些都是指整个印度铁路系统的其他方面，而不是这两条特定铁路连接后的结果。正确回答这个问题需要你准确捕捉数字信息，并将其与相应的铁路连接事件联系起来。

这道题的答案是"inspiration"，因为原文中明确提到这条路线是儒勒·凡尔纳写作《八十天环游地球》的灵感来源之一。在听力材料中，我们可以听到"it was part of the inspiration for French writer Jules Verne's book Around the World in Eighty Days"这句话。这里的"inspiration"正是我们需要填写的答案。题目要求填写一个单词，而"inspiration"恰好符合这个要求，既能准确表达原文的意思，又符合题目的填写规则。此外，"inspiration"（灵感）这个词在描述文学创作的背景时非常常用，它准确地表达了这条铁路线对于小说创作的启发作用。因此，"inspiration"是最合适的答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 36-40
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer

content: The network radiated inward from$15125$Madras, and Calcutta.
It was not long before various independent kingdoms had their own$15126$
When the war finished the railways were suffering from disrepair and$15127$
Between 1920 and 1929, the railways had a$15128$of around £687 million.
The rolling stock that was moved to the Middle East included locomotives and$15129$

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是Bombay（孟买），因为题目要求填写的是表格中1875-1899年期间铁路网向内辐射的起点城市之一。在原文中，我们可以找到关键信息："By 1880 the network had a route mileage of about 14,500 km, mostly radiating inward from the three major port cities of Bombay, Madras and Calcutta"。这里明确提到了三个主要港口城市，而Bombay是第一个被提到的城市。在填写表格时，我们通常按照原文中提到的顺序填写，因此Bombay是正确答案。此外，题目中的时间范围（1875-1899）也与原文中提到的1880年相符。所以，即使你不熟悉印度地理，只要注意听到"three major port cities"这个关键短语，并记住随后提到的第一个城市名，就能较容易地回答这个问题。

这道题的关键在于理解原文中"autonomous kingdoms began to have their own rail systems"这一句。题目中提到的"independent kingdoms"与原文中的"autonomous kingdoms"是同义表达，都指独立或自治的王国。文中明确指出这些王国开始拥有自己的"rail systems"，这正是我们需要填入空格的答案。注意，答案允许写"rail systems"或"railway systems"，因为这两个表达在这里是等同的。所以，虽然原文使用的是"rail systems"，但"railway systems"也被认为是正确答案。这个题目考察了你对同义词的理解能力，以及从长句中提取关键信息的能力。在听力练习中，要特别注意类似"began to have their own"这样的表达，它往往暗示着重要信息即将出现。

这道题的关键在于准确理解第一次世界大战后铁路系统的状况。文中提到"with the end of the war, the railways were in a state of disrepair and collapse"，这里的"collapse"是关键词。"Collapse"比"disrepair"表达了更严重的状况，意味着整个系统的崩溃或瘫痪，而不仅仅是损坏。这与题目要求描述的1907-1919年期间铁路的最终状况相符。此外，"collapse"作为名词可以直接填入表格中，符合题目要求的不超过两个词的答案格式。因此，"collapse"是最准确和恰当的答案。

items.answer: capital value / capital high / value / high
items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于准确理解"capital value"这个术语。在听力材料中，我们可以清楚地听到"the railways represented a capital value of some 687 million sterling"这句话。这里的"capital value"正是我们要填入的答案。它指的是铁路系统的资本价值或资产价值，而不是经济繁荣或其他经济指标。题目要求我们填写的是在1920-1929年期间铁路系统的具体特征，而不是整体经济状况。因此，虽然文中提到了"economic boom"，但这并不是我们要填的答案。记住，在做听力题时，要仔细听每一个细节，尤其是涉及具体数字和专业术语的部分。这里的"687 million sterling"就是一个重要的提示，帮助我们定位到正确的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解"rolling stock"这个专业术语，它指的是铁路上可移动的车辆设备。在原文中，作者列举了"rolling stock"的两个具体例子：locomotives（机车）和coaches（客车厢）。题目要求填写不超过两个词，因此正确答案是"coaches"。这里需要注意的是，虽然"locomotives"在句子中先被提到，但它不是正确答案，因为题目特别指出要填写被运往中东的rolling stock中包括机车和什么。所以，正确答案应该是"coaches"。这道题考察了学生对专业词汇的理解能力，以及对听力材料中细节信息的准确把握能力。要做对这道题，学生需要仔细听辨句子结构，并根据题目要求选择恰当的答案。
